title: Stenandrium floridanum
taxonomy: Stenandrium floridanum is classified within the family Acanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus family. This family includes around 2,964 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Stenandrium floridanum, specifically, is a species of flowering plant native to Florida in the United States. It belongs to the genus Stenandrium, which is comprised of several species native to the Americas. The classification of Stenandrium floridanum is further supported by its characteristics, such as its small white flowers and hairy leaves.

intro: Stenandrium floridanum, commonly known as the Florida snake root or false pennyroyal, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. Bioactive constituents of Stenandrium floridanum include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the latter being one of the most common herbal preparations made with Stenandrium floridanum, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
